description: Military units, formations, and martial organizations.
factions: Vanguard
The first warriors into battle, absorbing the enemy's fury so the main force can strike.
Militia
Armed citizens drilling between harvests, ready to defend their homes when professional soldiers are nowhere near.
Guard
Sworn protectors standing watch over a person, place, or treasure and answering threats with disciplined steel.
Watch
Patrol officers keeping order in streets and alleys, enforcing curfew and answering cries for help.
Patrol
Scouts sweeping roads and borderlands, reporting threats before they reach the settlements behind them.
Brigade
A mid-sized fighting force striking hard at a single objective under orders from higher command.
Regiment
Soldiers marching under a shared banner and proud tradition, their identity forged in past victories.
Battalion
Hundreds of troops operating as a self-sufficient fighting block, anchoring the line of battle.
Squadron
A tight formation of ships or riders maneuvering as one to deliver concentrated force where it matters most.
Platoon
A handful of soldiers training and fighting as a close-knit unit, trusting each other with their lives.
Squad
The smallest tactical unit, where every member's failure or courage decides the group's survival.
Inquisition
Zealous investigators rooting out heresy and corruption, wielding faith and fear in equal measure.
Crusade
Fervent warriors marching under a holy or ideological banner, leaving devastation in their righteous wake.
Outpost
A skeleton crew holding a remote position, watching the frontier with little hope of quick reinforcement.
Station
Personnel maintaining a fixed post for resupply, communication, or surveillance -- keeping vital networks running.
Post
A small garrison holding a checkpoint or waypoint, controlling who and what passes through.
Fort
Walls, towers, and a standing garrison anchoring military control over a contested stretch of land.
Fortress
Massive fortifications housing armies behind near-impregnable stone, daring any siege to try its strength.
Stronghold
A bastion of last resort where defenders dig in, prepared to hold against overwhelming odds.
Bastion
A fortified bulwark jutting forward to break enemy advances and shelter the forces behind it.
Redoubt
A hasty fortification thrown up where battle demands, offering desperate cover in the thick of fighting.
Garrison
Soldiers stationed permanently in a fortress, keeping order, repairing walls, and waiting for the next attack.
Arsenal
Racks of weapons and stores of powder filling a guarded depot, fueling the war effort.
Army
A nation's massed military force marching to war under unified command and projecting sovereign power abroad.
Inquisitors
Ruthless agents investigating heresy and supernatural threats, answering to divine law above any secular authority.
Knights
Armored warriors bound by oath to a lord or dark patron, riding to battle under a dreaded banner.
Sellswords
Hired blades loyal only to coin, fighting for whoever fills their purse.
War Band
Loosely bonded warriors raiding and fighting together, held by a warchief's charisma rather than formal rank.
Unit
Mounted combatants striking fast and withdrawing, using speed and shock to shatter slower formations.
Fleet
Warships and crews operating under an admiral's flag, projecting power across oceans and coastlines.
Corsairs
State-sanctioned raiders preying on enemy shipping and blending patriotism with piracy for profit.
Privateers
Licensed pirates hunting specific targets under government seal, skirting the line between law and plunder.
Wardens
Rangers patrolling the major roads and passes, keeping trade routes safe from bandits and beasts.
Jihad
A holy struggle mobilizing the faithful for spiritual or military conquest under divine imperative.
Cohort
A tightly drilled company of soldiers fighting as one disciplined block within a larger legion.
Legion
Thousands of soldiers marching under a storied name, their reputation alone enough to break an enemy's nerve.
Armada
A vast war fleet sailing under unified command, aiming to sweep all opposition from the seas.
Vanguards
Elite shock troops spearheading every assault and clearing the path for the forces that follow.
Riders
Mounted warriors specializing in swift raids and reconnaissance, striking before the enemy can react.
Force
A body of fighters assembled for a campaign, drawn together by duty or desperation.
Reserves
Trained soldiers waiting in readiness, called to arms only when the front line buckles.
Camp
A temporary gathering of warriors or travelers, humming with tension between marches and battles.
Corps
A specialized branch handling distinct duties -- diplomacy, engineering, or supply -- in support of the broader war effort.
Royal Guard
Handpicked warriors protecting the crown, sworn to die before any harm reaches their sovereign.
Rangers
Wilderness scouts patrolling the wilds, tracking threats and guiding travelers through dangerous frontier.
Military Division
A specialized arm of the military training for a focused combat role, deployed where its expertise matters most.
Division
A distinct branch within a larger body handling its own mandate, reporting up the chain of command.
Section
A small subunit carrying out narrow tasks inside a larger organization and executing precise orders.
